summary: Final docs gate on runtime2-callback. Coder XML doc quality on the new public surface (Callback/, Snapshot/, modules/callback/run.cs, modules/output/ask.cs, PlangDataSerializer, *.Snapshot.cs partials) was already high — no member-level fills needed. Wrote architecture docs for the new callback + snapshot subsystems, three cross-cutting good_to_know entries, user-facing module docs (callback new, output rewrite, index update), and applied all four character proposals filed on the branch. CHANGELOG drafted in v1/result.md.

changes.description: Full architecture + developer reference for the callback subsystem: ICallback contract, AskCallback vs ErrorCallback wire-shape choice, seal-then-verify gate (S-F1), size caps (S-F3), sensitive-property strip (S-F4), %!ask.answer% resume sentinel, lazy-Signature ICallback carve-out, application/plang+data mimetype.

ISnapshotted classifier, Snapshot.@this section-tree container, per-subsystem subtree reference (10 implementers), referent-integrity discipline, what's NOT in the snapshot, wire-shape choice rationale.
Three new entries: lazy Data.Signature ICallback-only carve-out (trip-wires are subtle); RestoredFrame is surrogate not Call.@this; Errors.Push sets error.App for callback materialisation.
Added Snapshot/Restore + Callbacks sections after Error Handling, each pointing to the topic file. Architecture remains a high-level map; topic files carry the detail.
User-facing reference for - run %callback%. Worked example, full typed-error key table, wire size limits, relationship to ask.

Rewrote with ask as its own action subsection. Covers vars: annotation and the resume mechanism in plain terms ('tampered or unsigned envelopes never resume'). Note: PLang .goal examples beyond the basic ones are tester turf.
Added callback row to the Core module reference table; expanded output's actions to include ask.

CHANGELOG entry drafted: Added (callbacks subsystem, callback.run, ask vars+resume, application/plang+data, App snapshot/restore, wire caps, sensitive strip), Renamed (SignedData → Signature), Internal (lazy-Signature carve-out, RestoredFrame surrogate, Errors.Push back-ref).

claude_md_proposals.reason: v2 — review-server workflow with HTTP API surface. Real tooling built on this branch; without persistent instructions future architect sessions would edit comments.json by hand. Canonical, not branch-specific.

v3 — test-designer prep two-files convention (test-strategy.md narrative + test-coverage.md matrix). Validated on this branch when test-designer pulled and surfaced the gap; codifying prevents the next branch repeating it.

v4 — stale .test.goal stubs are part of stage closure. User explicitly flagged the incident; concrete rule with explicit out-of-scope escape hatch. Falls under architect/test-designer/coder proposal exception.

v1 — read-only scope. User flagged the 'auditor changed code' incident on this branch (codeanalyzer fixed F1/F2 in AskCallback.cs without being asked). Tight one-paragraph rule, reviewer-bot exception applies.

character_proposals.reason: v2 — review server is real tooling that needs persistent instructions to be reused next session.
v3 — two-file test-designer prep convention validated on this branch.
v4 — branch-incident-driven, addresses real failure mode (stale stubs) with concrete steps.
v1 — branch-incident-driven; reviewer-bot must not change code.
